title: tapinanthus ehlersii
intro: Tapinanthus ehlersii, also known as the dwarf mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of other trees, specifically the Acacia species in Southern Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Tapinanthus ehlersii are thought to include the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Herbal preparations of Tapinanthus ehlersii are usually made from its leaves and stems, which are dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
